changes.result: CRITICAL FINDING — IMPACTS RESOLUTION PATHS FOR 3 OF 9 BIOMARKER-BLOCKED CANDIDATES
changes.finding: CONFIRMED from reading validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py lines 105-110. Function load_ssot_keys() returns set(biomarkers.keys()) — TOP-LEVEL CANONICAL KEY NAMES ONLY. Aliases are NOT traversed. This means biomarker IDs that exist as aliases but not as canonical top-level keys will be reported as 'not in SSOT'. Three blocked IDs are confirmed aliases in biomarkers.yaml: (1) hgb is registered as alias for canonical key hemoglobin at backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml line 734. (2) plt is registered as alias for canonical key platelets at biomarkers.yaml line 780. (3) wbc is registered as alias for canonical key white_blood_cells at biomarkers.yaml lines 765-766. CONSEQUENCE: Adding these as additional aliases to biomarkers.yaml would be a NO-OP. The validator does not use aliases. The ONLY valid resolution for hgb/plt/wbc identity blocks is Phase 4: create production PSI copies using canonical key names (hemoglobin, platelets, white_blood_cells) instead of the alias IDs. Cursor must NOT attempt SSOT alias additions for these three.
changes.citation: backend/scripts/validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py:105-110 (load_ssot_keys: set(biomarkers.keys())); backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:734 (hgb alias under hemoglobin); backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:780 (plt alias under platelets); backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:765-766 (wbc alias under white_blood_cells)

changes.finding: All 9 blocked candidates verified against biomarkers.yaml canonical keys and production package existence. (1) urea_high_prerenal: blocker=hgb (supporting). hgb IS alias for hemoglobin (biomarkers.yaml:734). Production package EXISTS (pkg_kb52c_urea_high_prerenal_volume_depletion_or_catabolic_load). Safe: hemoglobin substitution in production PSI. (2) non_hdl_cholesterol_high: blocker=non_hdl (primary). non_hdl is NOT in biomarkers.yaml as key or alias. Canonical is non_hdl_cholesterol (biomarkers.yaml:149, 'Non-HDL cholesterol (TC - HDL)'). Production package EXISTS (pkg_kb52d_non_hdl_cholesterol_high_atherogenic_lipoprotein_burden). Staged PSI package_id matches (generated_pilot PSI line 4: pkg_kb52d_non_hdl_cholesterol_high_atherogenic_lipoprotein_burden). Safe: non_hdl_cholesterol substitution. (3) hematocrit_high_absolute: blockers=erythropoietin, jak2_v617f. Neither in biomarkers.yaml. erythropoietin = new biomarker addition required. jak2_v617f = genetic marker outside current SSOT scope. No production package exists. STOP gate 2 fires. (4) hematocrit_high_relative: blocker=erythropoietin. Not in SSOT. No production package exists. STOP gate 2 fires. (5) mcv_low_microcytosis: blocker=rbc_count. Not a canonical key or alias; canonical is rbc (biomarkers.yaml:917, 'Red blood cell count'). No production package exists (only hematocrit_low package found). STOP gate 2 fires: no host package. (6) plt_high_reactive: blockers=plt (primary), wbc (supporting). Both are aliases for platelets, white_blood_cells (see stage_2a.validator_ssot_lookup_behaviour_critical). Production package EXISTS. Safe: canonical substitution. (7) plt_low_consumption: blockers=plt (primary), wbc (supporting). Same analysis. Production package EXISTS. Safe: canonical substitution. (8) lym_low_lymphopenia: blockers=lym (ambiguous: lymphocytes or lymphocyte_pct?), wbc_total (not in SSOT), medical-review carry-forward, system-mapping carry-forward. STOP gate 2 fires: ambiguous mapping. Scope exclusion per prompt line 44 (medical-review blockers out of scope). No production package exists. (9) wbc_high_reactive: blockers=wbc primary (wbc=alias for white_blood_cells, but medical-review AND system-mapping carry-forward present). STOP gate 2 fires: medical-review carry-forward. Scope exclusion. No production package.
changes.citation: docs/sprints/beta_readiness/P1-14_activation_readiness_cohort_manifest.yaml:133-209 (all 9 blocked items); backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:149 (non_hdl_cholesterol), :370 (urea), :734 (hemoglobin aliases), :765-766 (white_blood_cells aliases), :780 (platelets aliases), :917 (rbc); knowledge_bus/packages/ directory listing (production package existence confirmed)

changes.finding: CONFIRMED. Behavioural surface: backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml changes would affect runtime SSOT canonicalisation lookups. Production PSI content changes (identity normalisation only) would affect PSI artefacts loaded by downstream tooling. Sprint explicitly prohibits: activating PSI at runtime (prompt line 131); changing activation logic (line 306); changing medical claims (line 303). Identity normalisation in production PSI copies is the ONLY permitted content change. No Intelligence Core module (backend/core/) is touched.

Regression targets per Phase 6: (1) validate_knowledge_package.py for each changed production package. (2) validate_promoted_signal_intelligence.py for each new production PSI. (3) validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py — must not regress; any unexpected regression triggers STOP gate 5 (prompt lines 367-373). If SSOT aliases are added, run any existing SSOT/biomarker validation script discoverable without adding new code (prompt line 362). Cursor must NOT modify any validator in this sprint (prompt line 363).

If SSOT aliases are added to biomarkers.yaml, this affects runtime canonicalisation for any consumer of the SSOT alias lookup. The sprint prohibits adding aliases for hgb/plt/wbc (already present). If non_hdl → non_hdl_cholesterol resolution is done via SSOT alias addition rather than PSI content normalisation, it would add a new alias that affects runtime. NOTE: For non_hdl, the safer approach is production PSI canonical ID substitution (non_hdl → non_hdl_cholesterol), NOT SSOT alias addition. This avoids any SSOT behavioural surface entirely. Cursor should prefer PSI canonical ID substitution over SSOT changes wherever a production package exists.

CONFIRMED. For each production PSI created, ONLY biomarker_id substitutions are permitted (alias → canonical key, or PSI primary metric non_hdl → non_hdl_cholesterol). ALL OTHER FIELDS must be byte-for-byte identical to the staged PSI. Any discrepancy in activation logic, thresholds, evidence strength, supporting marker relationships, override rules, contradiction markers, or interpretation text triggers STOP gate 3 (prompt lines 303-315) and the candidate must be blocked. Cursor must read the full staged PSI before writing the production copy and do a field-by-field comparison.

CONFIRMED. For any candidate where production PSI content differs from staged PSI (identity normalisation only), prompt lines 293-300 require an explicit field-level diff in the sprint report: old ID, new ID, reason, SSOT evidence, confirmation medical meaning unchanged. This is mandatory for urea_high_prerenal (hgb→hemoglobin), non_hdl_high (non_hdl→non_hdl_cholesterol), plt_high_reactive (plt→platelets, wbc→white_blood_cells), plt_low_consumption (plt→platelets, wbc→white_blood_cells).

changes.finding: CONFIRMED. PSI is non-user-facing and non-runtime unless explicitly activated later (P1-15 baseline per prompt line 42). This sprint must not activate PSI at runtime (prompt line 131). No frontend, Gemini, DTO, report compiler, scoring or orchestrator files may be touched (prompt lines 105-114). Any biomarker opt-in that would cause runtime/user-facing behaviour triggers STOP gate 4 (prompt line 342).

overall_verdict: HARDENED. All 5 front-matter fields confirmed valid. All mandatory authority files confirmed present. STOP gate 1 pre-confirmed to fire for all 4 package-identity candidates: staged PSI internal package_id = pkg_kb52c_*; production packages exist only as pkg_kb58_*; no pkg_kb52c_rbc_*/rdw_cv_* production packages exist; no identity-normalisation tooling in repository; cross-ID placement rejected by GPT in P1-15. STOP gate 2 pre-confirmed for 5 of 9 biomarker-identity candidates (hematocrit_high_absolute, hematocrit_high_relative, mcv_low_microcytosis: erythropoietin/jak2_v617f absent from SSOT, no production packages; lym_low, wbc_high_reactive: medical-review carry-forward out of P1-16 scope, ambiguous lym identity, no production packages). 4 of 9 biomarker-identity candidates pre-confirmed as resolvable via production PSI canonical ID normalisation (urea_high_prerenal, non_hdl_high, plt_high_reactive, plt_low_consumption): production packages exist, identity corrections are one-to-one, no medical meaning change. CRITICAL FINDING: Validator uses canonical keys only — hgb/plt/wbc are already aliases in SSOT; do NOT add redundant alias entries; use canonical IDs in production PSI copies only. Change_type MIXED and risk_level HIGH confirmed correct. Two mandatory hardening observations surfaced: (1) validator alias-vs-canonical-key distinction; (2) prefer production PSI canonical substitution over SSOT edits where a production package exists.
